items.headline: Pakistan raises fuel prices by up to 55 percent as global oil shock intensifies
items.body: Pakistan increased petrol by 43 percent and diesel by 55 percent, the second hike in a month. The move exposes inability to sustain fuel subsidies, risking accelerated inflation for 240 million people.

items.headline: Malaysia orders government workers to stay home as oil crisis forces energy rationing
items.body: Malaysia will implement work-from-home policies for public sector employees starting April 15 to reduce fuel consumption. The policy signals expectations of protracted conflict and marks shift from crisis management to structural demand reduction.

items.headline: Federal forces seize control of Baidoa as Somalia's constitutional crisis deepens
items.body: Somali federal forces took full control of Baidoa following days of fighting, forcing regional president to resign and flee. The federal government appointed an acting leader while restricting media access, escalating tensions over constitutional amendments regional states reject.

items.headline: Earthquake strikes Indonesia, killing one and triggering brief tsunami warning
items.body: A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck North Maluku, killing at least one person and injuring several others. Tsunami waves up to 75 centimeters were recorded before warnings were lifted, with authorities still assessing damage in remote villages.

Far-right parties now sit in a third of OECD governments, reshaping international funding priorities
